## 20260415_141734 — SESSION-022 Meta Resolution

```
======================================================================
  META-RESOLUTION REPORT
======================================================================
  Session                : SESSION-022
  Problem class          : repeated_audit_and_duplicate_building
  Observed issue         : New conversations were at risk of re-reading old material,
                           re-auditing the same gap, and re-planning already-known work
                           instead of pushing the next production bottleneck.

  Resolution             : Added SessionGuard fingerprinting and duplicate-work checks.
  Resolution scope       : Cross-session task registration, repetition risk reporting,
                           explicit handoff refresh, and stronger next-session priority order.

  Future avoidance rule  : Do not run another broad project audit unless a subsystem has
                           materially changed. Read SESSION_HANDOFF.md, PROJECT_BRAIN.json,
                           DEDUP_REGISTRY.json, and SESSION_PROTOCOL.md first.

  Current next bottleneck: Character evolution/search loop, not another generic audit.
======================================================================
```

## 20260415_143131 — SESSION-023 meta note

```text
======================================================================
  SESSION META ANTI-STAGNATION NOTE
======================================================================
  Observation            : Character output had already escaped the pure-demo phase,
                           but the next risk was continuing to add packaging layers
                           without giving characters a real search loop.

  Action taken           : Added a baseline character evolution/search stage before
                           pack export, plus a persistent search artifact
                           (*_character_evolution.json).

  Remaining risk         : Future sessions may still over-focus on wrappers, exports,
                           or broad audits while leaving character-specific objective
                           metrics weak.

  Mandatory next focus   : Prefer Character Evolution 2.0 (silhouette/readability/
                           state distinction/diversity/stagnation recovery) before
                           doing more superficial presentation work.

  Efficiency rule        : Do not repeat another broad project audit unless a major
                           subsystem changed materially. Read SESSION_HANDOFF.md and
                           PROJECT_BRAIN.json first, then continue from the highest-
                           value unresolved P1 task.
======================================================================
```

## SESSION-024 Governance Note — Character Evolution 2.0 Foundation

This session addressed a higher-order stagnation risk: **character evolution existed, but it could still behave like a shallow local search with weak recovery**. That pattern would have produced repeated future sessions that kept “tuning search” without materially increasing the system's ability to escape mediocre basins.

The mitigation added in SESSION-024 is structural rather than cosmetic:

| Risk | Governance Action | Expected Effect |
|------|-------------------|-----------------|
| Character search collapsing around one incumbent | Diverse elite pool added | Preserves alternative high-value candidates |
| Search progress flatlining for several iterations | Adaptive variation strength added | Increases exploration pressure when local progress stalls |
| Recovery never escaping the same basin | Restart-mode candidate injection added | Gives the search a controlled way to re-open exploration |
| Search quality hard to audit later | Objective weights, strength history, and stagnation events exported | Future sessions can see why a search got stuck or improved |

The key anti-stagnation rule for future sessions is therefore:

> Do **not** treat character evolution as solved just because it now has better scoring and restart behavior. The next non-cosmetic step must expand the **semantic mutation space** and define **benchmark asset targets**; otherwise later work risks becoming another loop of local tuning without real production-quality gains.
